title: Star Wars: The Han Solo Trilogy

'''''Star Wars: The Han Solo Trilogy''''' is a [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] novel series written by author [[A. C. Crispin]]. The trilogy includes the books ''[[The Paradise Snare]]'' ([[1997]]), ''[[The Hutt Gambit]]'' (1997), and ''[[Rebel Dawn]]'' ([[1998]]), which tell the backstory of [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] from his early years right up to the moment he walks into the [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|cantina]] during the events of the film [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']].<ref name="Penguin" />

==Contents==
*''[[The Paradise Snare]]'' {{C|[[The Paradise Snare (abridged audiobook)|abridged audiobook]]}}, [[May 5]], [[1997]]<ref name="Paradise Snare" />
*''[[The Hutt Gambit]]'' {{C|[[The Hutt Gambit (abridged audiobook)|abridged audiobook]]}}, [[August 11]], 1997<ref name="Hutt Gambit">''[[The Hutt Gambit]]''</ref>
*''[[Rebel Dawn]]'' {{C|[[Rebel Dawn (abridged audiobook)|abridged audiobook]]}}, [[March 9]], [[1998]]<ref name="Rebel Dawn" />

==Development==
During the development of ''Star Wars: The Han Solo Trilogy'', author [[A. C. Crispin]] was requested and instructed by [[Lucasfilm]] to not cover [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]'s time at the [[Imperial Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]] or his first encounter with [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]].<ref name="TheForce.net">{{WebCite|url=https://www.theforce.net/jedicouncil/interview/crispin.asp|text=A.C. Crispin|work=[https://theforce.net TheForce.net]|archivedate=20200803034451}}</ref> Despite this, she was allowed to write a brief explanation of how Han had saved Chewie's life when he was enslaved by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]], and had gotten cashiered for his pains with a [[Life debt/Legends|life debt]]. Crispin was also faced with the challenge of trying to explain how Han discovered his cousin, [[Thrackan Sal-Solo|Thracken Sal Solo]], without ever knowing anything about [[Dalla Solo|his]] [[Jonash Solo|parents]], even their names.<ref name="ACCess 2013">{{WordPress|subdomain=accrispin|url=2013/02/09/our-12th-anniversary-and-disneys-plans-for-a-han-solo-film|text=Our 12th Anniversary, and Disney’s plans for a Han Solo Film|name=ACCess|archivedate=20160412145409}}</ref>

The three books were originally released individually. They were later rereleased together as a [[Science Fiction Book Club]] edition by the title [[The Han Solo Trilogy (hardcover)|''The Han Solo Trilogy'']]<ref name="Trilogy">[[The Han Solo Trilogy (hardcover)|''The Han Solo Trilogy'' hardcover]]</ref> and as an audiobook by the title ''The Han Solo Omnibus''.<ref name="Omnibus">[[The Han Solo Omnibus|''The Han Solo Omnibus'' abridged audiobook collection]]</ref> The Sci-Fi Book Club edition is missing the epilogue of ''The Hutt Gambit''. <ref name="Trilogy" />

Crispin became known for creating fascinating "backstory" for media icons, such as Han Solo in ''The Han Solo Trilogy'' as well as [[w:c:memory-alpha:Spock|Mr. Spock]]'s father [[w:c:memory-alpha:Sarek|Sarek]] in ''[[w:c:memory-alpha:Sarek (novel)|Star Trek: Sarek]]''. When an editor working for [[The Walt Disney Company|Disney]] was seeking an author to write a novel dealing with the backstory of [[w:c:pirates:Jack Sparrow|Captain Jack Sparrow]] in ''[[Wikipedia:Pirates of the Caribbean|Pirates of the Caribbean]]'', they contacted Crispin's agent and contracted her to write ''[[w:c:pirates:Pirates of the Caribbean: The Price of Freedom|Pirates of the Caribbean: The Price of Freedom]]'' after reading ''The Han Solo Trilogy''.<ref name="TheForce.net Sparrow">{{WebCite|url=http://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-jc-lit-forum-reviews-retrospective-special-han-solo-trilogy-iii-rebel-dawn.29948157/#post-30205333|text=The JC Lit Forum Reviews RETROSPECTIVE Special: Han Solo Trilogy III: Rebel Dawn|author=[[A. C. Crispin]]|date=2009-06-27|work=[https://theforce.net TheForce.net]|archivedate=20131002015619}}</ref> When releasing the fifth excerpt, Crispin spoke how she was privileged to write the scene where Han Solo first beheld—and fell for—the ''[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|Millennium Falcon]]'' in ''The Han Solo Trilogy''. She assured fans it was every bit as much of a thrill to write the scene with Jack Sparrow and the ''[[w:c:pirates:Black Pearl|Wicked Wench]]'', the [[w:c:pirates:East India Trading Company|East India Trading Company]] merchant vessel that became the pirate ship ''Black Pearl''.<ref name="TPoF Excerpt 5">{{WebCite|url=http://www.accrispin.com/The%20Price%20of%20Freedom%20excerpt%2005.pdf|text=The Price of Freedom excerpt 05.pdf – Chapter 6|author=[[A. C. Crispin|Ann Crispin]]|date=2011-03-15|format=PDF|archivedate=20110903052943}}</ref>

A Barnes & Noble Collectible Edition leather-bound hardcover was released on [[October 20]], [[2017]].<ref name="BnN">{{BN|url=w/star-wars-a-c-crispin/1125814088|text=Star Wars: The Han Solo Trilogy (Barnes & Noble Collectible Editions)}}</ref>
